action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 13's CLIP ViT experiment failed catastrophically (val_acc ~19%, bacc ~23%). The proven best remains iteration 9's ConvNeXt-Tiny with natural CE + layer-wise LR + drop_path=0.1 for 20 epochs, followed by post-hoc logit adjustment (tau=1.5), scoring 0.8514. Analysis: Instead of chasing a weaker architecture, I should improve the strong ConvNeXt model via a model soup: train a second ConvNeXt with the exact same hyperparameters but a different seed (123), then grid-search the interpolation weight (alpha) that maximizes validation balanced accuracy when merging the two state dicts. Averaging weights of two fine-tuned models often finds a broader minimum that generalizes better. After merging, I'll tune tau and evaluate. Decision: 1) Rewrite train.py cleanly with the proven recipe and seed argument. 2) Write tune_soup.py to search alpha ∈ {0.3,0.4,0.5,0.6,0.7} and save the best merged checkpoint. 3) Train seed=123 model, run soup tuning, run tau tuning, smoke-test predict.py, and submit eval—all in one chained command.
action.command: python train.py --seed 123 --output_name convnext_s2.pt && python tune_soup.py --ckpt2 checkpoints/convnext_s2.pt --output checkpoints/soup.pt && python tune_tau.py --checkpoint checkpoints/soup.pt --output checkpoints/soup_adj.pt && mkdir -p /tmp/fake_test && cp /data/val.pt /tmp/fake_test/test.pt && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/fake_test --checkpoint checkpoints/soup_adj.pt --output /tmp/fake_test/out.json && python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/tmp/fake_test/out.json')); print('Smoke test OK, preds:', len(d['predictions']))"
